title: Build a Response Envelope
prompt_md: A handler returns a *response*: a status code plus a body. Write `build_response(data)` that wraps any `data` value into the dict `{"status": 200, "body": data}`. This mirrors what a framework returns when you `return data` from a `GET` handler — a `200 OK` carrying your payload.
hints: Return a dict with two keys: "status" and "body".
"status" is always the integer 200 here; "body" is whatever was passed in.
data can be any JSON value: a dict, a list, a number, etc. Don't inspect it — just store it.
